items.t: — the quiet week breaks a session early: +1.33% late Tuesday on a $3,998.80–$4,142.70 run, short covering and haven demand doing the lifting
— the high of the day knocks on sixty for the first time since the June selloff; the board settles 59.64, +1.67%
— the NUM condemns De Beers' two-year pause, counters with executive cost reviews and retraining, and pledges every legal channel
— a 25% Section 301 tariff stacks on the existing 10% at 12:01 a.m. July 23; rough gems and unwrought metals exempt, polished and finished jewelry pay
— April–June retail sales up 32%, gold same-store +50%; diamond same-store −17% group and −58% on the mainland; 148 net stores closed
— retail value up 16% for the quarter, same-store +17% in China and +30% in Hong Kong & Macau; 776 doors after 49 net closures
— the 2026 Real Weddings study says 61% of couples married in 2025 chose lab-grown center stones; premium 1ct labs retail $800–1,200
— lab-grown collections reach Spain and Italy via Barcelona and Milan flagships, the eighth market for the line; the NDC disputes its carbon math
— a 1970s Cartier Bamboo Coussin leads a record spring as Piaget doubles and triples; a 1930s Rolex Prince manages $2,176
— a fourth Rolex-anchored Loupe boutique takes over Sheffield's 165-year-old H. L. Brown showroom at 2 Barker's Pool
